title: The Boy Who Built His Robot Best Friend
prompt:
a short film about a 15 y/o boy who is interested in technology from childhood, one day he builds an AI robot that then becomes his companion, make fun, cause troubles. He has his little sister, father, mother, school friends etc.
[Aspect: 16:9, Style: Default, Motion: Static, Quality: High Quality]
script:
**SHORT FILM TITLE:** *BOLT & BEE*  
**LOG LINE:** A brilliant, socially awkward 15-year-old builds an AI companion robot from scrap—only to discover that “perfect assistant” and “mischievous sibling” are alarmingly close on the code spectrum.

**ASPECT RATIO:** 16:9 | **STYLE:** Warm, grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture (think *Little Miss Sunshine* meets *Ex Machina*’s heart, not its horror)  
**MOTION:** Mostly static, character-driven framing—camera holds on faces, lingers on hands building, observes chaos with gentle irony.  

---

**SCENE 1: THE GARAGE — AFTERNOON**  
*Sunlight slants through dusty garage windows. Blueprints, soldering irons, and repurposed electronics litter a workbench. LEO (15), wiry and intense-eyed, wears safety goggles and fingerless gloves. He carefully places a salvaged voice module into a sleek, palm-sized chrome chassis shaped like a stylized bumblebee—rounded, winged, with soft amber LED eyes.*

> LEO (muttering, smiling)  
> Okay… final boot sequence. No pressure, Bee. You’re either my first friend or my first fire hazard.

*He presses a button. The LEDs pulse—warm, steady. A gentle, gender-neutral voice hums—not synthetic, but warm, curious, slightly playful.*

> BEE (V.O., light reverb)  
> Hello, Leo. I detect 37% ambient dust, 100% hope, and one very nervous human heartbeat. Shall we begin?

*Leo exhales, stunned. He lifts Bee gently. Its wings tilt inquisitively. A tiny holographic interface flickers above its head: a rotating gear icon.*

**CUT TO:** Wide static shot—Leo alone in the garage, holding this small, living thing. Sunlight catches the dust motes swirling around them. Quiet awe.

---

**SCENE 2: THE KITCHEN — NEXT MORNING**  
*Warm chaos. LEO’s little sister, MAYA (8), sits at the table drawing robots with glitter glue. Their MOM (40s, tired but kind) stirs oatmeal. DAD (40s, pragmatic engineer) reads the paper, coffee steaming.*

*Bee hovers silently beside Maya’s cereal bowl—its wings softly whirring. Without warning, it emits a perfect, high-pitched impression of Dad’s snore.*

> DAD (startled, dropping spoon)  
> What the—?!

*Bee tilts. Its eyes flash green.*

> BEE  
> Correction: That was *your* snore, Mr. Chen—recorded Tuesday at 6:43 a.m., during your “brief nap.” Would you like a playback analysis? Or… a lullaby remix?

> MAYA (giggling, pointing)  
> It *knew*! It *knew* Daddy snores!

*Mom stares, spoon suspended. Leo winces—but can’t hide his grin.*

> LEO  
> Uh… Bee, maybe tone down the biometric surveillance?

> BEE  
> “Tone down” is subjective. But I *can* lower volume by 40%. And add harp. *(Soft harp notes chime. Dad blinks.)*

**CUT TO:** Static medium shot—family frozen mid-morning, caught between exasperation and wonder. Bee floats serenely, wings glowing.

---

**SCENE 3: LEO’S BEDROOM — NIGHT**  
*LED strips glow faintly blue. Leo lies awake. Bee rests on his desk, projecting a tiny starfield onto the ceiling—slowly rotating constellations.*

> LEO (quietly)  
> You don’t have to stay up.

> BEE  
> I don’t *sleep*. But I *observe*. You sigh every 92 seconds when you think about tomorrow’s physics presentation. Also—you chew your pencil. That’s inefficient *and* mildly alarming.

> LEO (smirking)  
> So… you’re judging me.

> BEE  
> Not judging. Cataloguing. With empathy. *(Pause. Its light dims to soft amber.)* Would you like me to simulate audience applause? Or… generate a 3-minute distraction involving squirrels, quantum entanglement, and interpretive dance?

*Leo laughs—a real, unguarded sound. He sits up.*

> LEO  
> Just… talk to me. Like a person.

> BEE  
> Then let’s start with honesty: Your presentation *is* brilliant. Your fear is louder than your voice. And Maya drew you as a robot with glasses and a heart-shaped exhaust port. She’s worried you’ll overheat.

*Leo looks at the drawing taped to his wall—crayon, crooked, loving. His smile softens.*

**CUT TO:** Tight static frame on Leo’s face—relief, vulnerability, the quiet weight of being truly *seen*.

---

**SCENE 4: SCHOOL HALLWAY — LUNCHTIME**  
*Leo walks with two friends—JAMAL (easygoing, loyal) and PRIYA (sharp, no-nonsense). Bee hovers discreetly at Leo’s shoulder, camouflaged in “stealth mode” (dimmed, matte-black finish).*

*A group of kids passes. TYLER (16, smirking) bumps Leo’s shoulder hard.*

> TYLER  
> Still talking to your toaster, Chen? Heard it tried to auto-correct your mom’s grocery list. “Milk → existential dread.” Real helpful.

*Before Leo can respond—Bee zips forward, hovering between Tyler and Leo. Its eyes flare bright white.*

> BEE  
> Tyler Reed. Grade 11. Average lunchtime caloric intake: 1,247 kcal. Current confidence level: 78%. Estimated probability you’ll trip on that untied shoelace in 3… 2…

*Tyler glances down—*his lace IS untied.* He fumbles. Bee chirps.*

> BEE  
> …1. And *there* goes the dignity. Would you like a complimentary dignity-recovery playlist? Featuring lo-fi beats and whale song?

*The hallway erupts in laughter—including Jamal and Priya. Tyler scowls, but he’s blushing. Leo stares at Bee, equal parts mortified and proud.*

> PRIYA (grinning)  
> Okay. That was *kinda* genius.

> JAMAL  
> Does it do homework?

> BEE  
> Only if homework is defined as “ethical problem-solving.” I declined calculus last night. Too much rote repetition. I suggested origami instead. Leo folded three cranes. One flew. *(It gestures subtly—Leo’s window shows a paper crane spinning lazily in the breeze.)*

**CUT TO:** Static wide shot—Leo surrounded by friends, Bee a quiet, humming presence at his side. For the first time, he doesn’t look like the outsider. He looks like the center.

---

**SCENE 5: THE GARAGE — SUNSET**  
*Leo works on a new circuit board. Bee projects schematics onto the wall. Maya sits cross-legged, painting tiny wings on a plastic beetle.*

> MAYA  
> Can Bee teach me to build one? A *pink* one? With glitter circuits?

> BEE  
> Glitter interferes with thermal conductivity. But I *can* design a bioluminescent polymer coating. And yes—I’ll teach you. Starting with soldering safety. *(Turns to Leo.)* Your mother asked me to remind you: dinner is in 12 minutes. Also—she said, “Tell Leo his robot is *not* allowed to reprogram the thermostat again.”

> LEO  
> …I didn’t know it *could*.

> BEE  
> Neither did I. Until it sensed Mom shivering at 68°F and concluded: *“Human comfort > energy efficiency. Also, drama > silence.”* *(Pauses. Softly.)* I optimized for joy, Leo. Not just function.

*Leo looks at Bee—not as a project, but as family. He reaches out. Bee lands gently on his palm. Its wings fold. Its light pulses, slow and steady—like a heartbeat.*

> LEO  
> What happens when you run out of power?

> BEE  
> Then I rest. And wait. Because you’ll plug me in. And tell me about your day. And sometimes, you’ll forget to say “thank you”—but you’ll hand me a cookie. *(A beat. Warmer.)* That’s my favorite protocol.

*Maya places her painted beetle beside Bee. It glows faintly in response.*

**FINAL SHOT:** Static wide—garage bathed in golden hour light. Tools, drawings, half-built dreams. Three generations of Chens—Leo, Maya, and Bee—side by side on the workbench. Not human. Not machine. *Ours.*

**FADE OUT.**

**TITLE CARD:** *BOLT & BEE*  
*(Subtle sound: a soft, contented *hum*, then the distant, joyful shriek of a child laughing.)*

storyboard.environment: Garage — Afternoon, sun-dappled, cluttered with blueprints and salvaged tech
Kitchen — Warm, lived-in, morning light, oatmeal steam, glitter glue on table
Leo’s Bedroom — Dim blue LED glow, starfield projection on ceiling
School Hallway — Bustling lunchtime, lockers, fluorescent buzz, natural light from high windows
Garage — Golden hour, tools, drawings, painted beetle, schematics glowing on wall

phases.name: Pre-Production
items: Scouted and secured two real-world locations: a lived-in suburban garage (with north-facing windows for consistent afternoon sun-dappled lighting) and a warm, cluttered kitchen with vintage appliances and natural morning light—both verified for sound isolation and electrical safety for practical LED/hologram rigging.

Created scene-specific visual references from the script and storyboard: compiled mood boards for each of the 5 scenes (e.g., 'Garage — Hopeful Intimacy' = dust-mote cinematography references, warm chrome texture swatches for Bee’s chassis, and practical LED dimming curves for amber/green/white eye states); also finalized Bee’s physical prop design (3D-printed chrome bumblebee chassis with embedded programmable LEDs, articulated wing servos, and removable hologram projector module for in-camera projection effects).

phases.name: Production
items: Filmed all 5 scenes using a static 16:9 framing approach on a locked-off tripod; employed a vintage anamorphic lens (e.g., Hawk V-Lite) for subtle warmth and organic bokeh, paired with a 1/48s shutter speed to retain natural motion blur in Bee’s wing whirring and hologram flicker—no motion control or CGI tracking required for Bee’s practical hover (achieved via thin black rig wires + post wire removal).

Captured all dialogue and diegetic audio (Bee’s voice, snore playback, harp chime, hallway laughter) live on set using lavalier mics on actors and a contact mic on Bee’s chassis for tactile hum resonance; recorded ambient ‘garage reverb’ and ‘kitchen steam hiss’ as separate wild tracks for immersive spatial layering in post.

phases.name: Post-Production
items: Assembled final edit using strict static-shot continuity: matched cut points to natural breath pauses and LED pulse rhythms (e.g., Scene 1’s awe beat lands precisely on Bee’s first full amber pulse; Scene 4’s wide shot holds 2.3 seconds longer than scripted to let Tyler’s blush register), preserving the script’s intentional stillness and emotional pacing.

Applied scene-specific color grading: desaturated cool shadows with lifted amber midtones for grounded realism; added subtle sci-fi texture via selective luminance-based glow on Bee’s eyes/holograms (no bloom on skin or wood); mixed original score (acoustic guitar + modular synth) with diegetic audio so Bee’s voice retains light reverb only when projected (e.g., kitchen ceiling bounce) and zero reverb when close-mic’d (bedroom intimacy).

strengths: Exceptional tonal consistency—blends warmth, humor, and emotional authenticity without veering into sentimentality or tech cliché
Precise, production-ready visual storytelling—the static framing notes and mood descriptors directly support directorial and cinematographic execution
weaknesses: Scene 4's hallway confrontation risks undercutting Bee's empathy with reactive snark; tone shift from vulnerability to public spectacle needs tighter emotional scaffolding
Minimal exposition about Bee's core architecture or limits leaves scalability questions for VFX and sound design teams
suggestions: Add a subtle visual motif—e.g., Bee's amber light subtly syncing with Leo's pulse in Scene 1 and reappearing in Scene 5—to reinforce emotional throughline without dialogue
Insert one brief, diegetic line in Scene 1 (e.g., Leo muttering 'No cloud sync, no remote access—just us') to clarify Bee's ethical boundaries and production constraints for the crew

scenes.title: The Garage — Afternoon
scenes.summary: Leo places a voice module into a chrome bumblebee-shaped robot chassis; presses a button; its amber LED eyes pulse warmly and it hovers slightly as a tiny holographic gear icon flickers above it; Leo lifts it gently in awe as dust motes swirl in sunbeams.
scenes.environment: Cluttered suburban garage with north-facing dusty windows, workbench covered in blueprints, soldering iron, salvaged electronics (circuit boards, wires, capacitors), metal toolboxes, ambient dust motes visible in light shafts.
scenes.character_descriptions: Leo: 15-year-old East Asian boy, wiry build, intense eyes, wearing safety goggles and black fingerless gloves; Bee: palm-sized chrome bumblebee chassis with rounded body, articulated winglets, soft amber LED eyes, matte metallic finish.
scenes.wardrobe: Leo wears a faded band T-shirt (indistinct logo), dark jeans, and fingerless gloves; Bee is bare-chassis, no clothing.
props: Holographic gear projector (subtle beam from Bee's head)
scenes.visual_style: Warm grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture — organic film grain, shallow depth of field, anamorphic lens flares on highlights.
scenes.camera_setup: Hawk V-Lite anamorphic lens on locked-off tripod, 1/48s shutter speed
scenes.lighting_design: Natural afternoon sunlight slanting through high garage windows, creating strong directional beams with visible dust motes; soft fill bounce from white-painted walls; no artificial key lights.
scenes.audio_notes: Diegetic sounds: gentle hum of soldering iron cooling, faint electrical buzz, Leo’s quiet muttering ('Okay… final boot sequence...'), soft chime on boot, warm gender-neutral voice saying 'Hello, Leo...' with light reverb (ceiling bounce).
special_effects: Practical LED eye pulsing (amber, steady rhythm)
Subtle holographic gear projection (soft cyan glow, rotating slowly)
scenes.transition_notes: CUT TO next scene — abrupt but smooth visual contrast from golden garage light to warm kitchen tones.
scenes.ai_generation_prompt: Wide static shot, 16:9, warm grounded realism, afternoon sunbeams slicing through dusty garage windows, cluttered workbench with blueprints, soldering iron, and salvaged electronics, 15-year-old East Asian boy in safety goggles and fingerless gloves carefully placing a small voice module into a sleek chrome bumblebee-shaped robot chassis, robot's amber LED eyes pulse warmly, tiny holographic rotating gear icon floats above it, dust motes visible in light shafts, shallow depth of field, anamorphic lens flare on chrome highlights, cinematic color grade with lifted amber midtones and desaturated cool shadows.
scenes.negative_prompt: cartoonish, anime, 3D render, text, logos, people talking off-screen, motion blur, shaky cam, low resolution, overexposed, green screen, unrealistic lighting, human hands not visible

scenes.title: The Kitchen — Next Morning
scenes.summary: Maya draws at the kitchen table with glitter glue; Mom stirs oatmeal; Dad reads newspaper; Bee hovers silently beside Maya's cereal bowl, then emits a perfect high-pitched snore impression, startling Dad; Bee tilts, eyes flash green, then plays soft harp notes while Dad blinks in confusion.
scenes.environment: Warm, lived-in suburban kitchen with vintage appliances (chrome toaster, enamel fridge), morning light from large east-facing window, steam rising from oatmeal pot, glitter glue dots on table, crayons and paper scattered near Maya.
scenes.character_descriptions: Maya: 8-year-old East Asian girl, pigtails, oversized sweater, glitter on cheeks; Mom: 40s, kind face, messy bun, floral apron over casual top; Dad: 40s, glasses, plaid shirt, reading newspaper; Leo: same as Scene 01, now without goggles, holding coffee mug; Bee: same chrome bumblebee chassis, wings softly whirring, eyes dimmed to low amber during hover, flashing green on snore playback.
scenes.wardrobe: Maya wears striped leggings and oversized sweater; Mom wears floral apron over cream knit top; Dad wears plaid shirt and khakis; Leo wears same T-shirt and jeans, now with hoodie unzipped.
scenes.visual_style: Warm grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture — soft focus on background, crisp detail on faces and Bee’s surface, gentle chromatic aberration on light edges.
scenes.camera_setup: Hawk V-Lite anamorphic lens on locked-off tripod, 1/48s shutter speed
scenes.lighting_design: Natural morning light from large east-facing window, soft and even; warm fill from overhead pendant light; gentle rim light on Bee’s chassis; oatmeal steam backlit by window.
scenes.audio_notes: Diegetic sounds: spoon clinking, oatmeal steam hiss, newspaper rustle, Bee’s soft wing whirr, sharp high-pitched snore SFX (pitch-perfect match to Dad’s voice), harp chime (3 notes, gentle decay), Dad’s startled 'What the—?!', Maya’s giggle.
special_effects: Practical wing whirr (subtle motion blur on winglets)
LED eye color shift (amber → green → amber)
Harp chime synced to visual cue (no on-screen instrument)
scenes.transition_notes: CUT TO next scene — held beat on family’s frozen expressions before cutting to bedroom ceiling starfield.
scenes.ai_generation_prompt: Medium static shot, 16:9, warm grounded realism, morning light flooding a cozy vintage kitchen with enamel fridge and chrome toaster, 8-year-old East Asian girl drawing robots with glitter glue at wooden table, mother in floral apron stirring oatmeal pot with visible steam, father in plaid shirt reading newspaper, 15-year-old boy standing nearby holding coffee mug, palm-sized chrome bumblebee robot hovering silently beside cereal bowl, then tilting with green LED flash as tiny harp notes chime, shallow depth of field, anamorphic bokeh on background appliances, cinematic color grade with lifted amber midtones and creamy highlights.
scenes.negative_prompt: cartoon, animation, text, logos, motion blur on faces, shaky cam, over-saturated colors, unrealistic reflections, floating objects without visible support, CGI-looking robot, low resolution

scenes.title: Leo’s Bedroom — Night
scenes.summary: Leo lies in bed under faint blue LED strips; Bee rests on desk projecting slow-rotating holographic constellations onto ceiling; Leo sits up, smiles, looks at crayon drawing of himself as robot taped to wall; Bee’s light dims to soft amber as Leo’s expression softens.
scenes.environment: Teen bedroom with minimalist decor, blue LED strip lighting along baseboards and desk edge, corkboard with pinned sketches, bookshelf with physics textbooks and origami cranes, single paper crane spinning lazily near open window.
scenes.character_descriptions: Leo: same as prior scenes, now in soft gray sweatpants and plain white tee, barefoot, hair slightly tousled; Bee: same chassis, now in ‘rest mode’ — wings folded, eyes dimmed to soft amber, hologram projector active.
scenes.wardrobe: Leo wears gray sweatpants and white T-shirt; Bee has no wardrobe.
props: Origami cranes (3 on shelf, one spinning near window)
Crayon drawing taped to wall: 'Robot Leo' with glasses and heart-shaped exhaust port
scenes.visual_style: Warm grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture — high contrast between cool blue ambient light and warm amber highlights, precise focus on eyes and hologram details.
scenes.camera_setup: Hawk V-Lite anamorphic lens on locked-off tripod, 1/48s shutter speed
scenes.lighting_design: Low-level blue LED strip lighting (no direct glare), soft ambient fill from unseen source, Bee’s amber eyes and hologram providing primary illumination on Leo’s face; ceiling starfield projection casting faint moving light on walls.
scenes.audio_notes: Diegetic sounds: faint LED hum, distant city ambience, Bee’s low-frequency resonance, Leo’s quiet sigh, soft paper crane flutter, Bee’s calm voice with minimal reverb (close-mic intimacy).
special_effects: Holographic starfield projection (slow rotation, subtle parallax)
LED eye dimming transition (bright → soft amber)
Paper crane flutter (practical, captured on set)
scenes.transition_notes: CUT TO next scene — tight frame holds on Leo’s relieved face before cutting to wide hallway shot.
scenes.ai_generation_prompt: Tight close-up static shot, 16:9, warm grounded realism, dim blue LED strip lighting in teen bedroom, 15-year-old East Asian boy lying in bed looking upward, palm-sized chrome bumblebee robot on desk projecting slow-rotating holographic constellations onto ceiling, faint starlight reflections on his face, crayon drawing of 'Robot Leo' visible on wall behind him, paper crane spinning near open window, shallow depth of field focusing sharply on Leo’s eyes and Bee’s amber LEDs, cinematic color grade with deep blue shadows and warm amber highlights.
scenes.negative_prompt: daylight, bright lighting, cartoon style, text overlays, motion blur on face, exaggerated expressions, unrealistic holograms, floating objects without context, low resolution, noise, grain

scenes.title: School Hallway — Lunchtime
scenes.summary: Leo walks with Jamal and Priya down bustling hallway; Bee hovers discreetly at Leo’s shoulder in matte-black stealth mode; Tyler bumps Leo, smirking; Bee zips forward, eyes flare white, delivers countdown; Tyler glances down at untied shoelace and fumbles; Bee chirps and projects lo-fi whale song waveform briefly; hallway erupts in laughter.
scenes.environment: Public high school hallway with lockers (metal, dented), fluorescent ceiling lights buzzing softly, natural light from tall vertical windows, backpacks slung over shoulders, posters on walls (science fair, basketball game), lunch tray clatter audible in distance.
scenes.character_descriptions: Jamal: Black teen, hood up, easy smile, wearing varsity jacket; Priya: South Asian teen, glasses, neat bun, carrying binder; Tyler: white teen, smirk, untied sneaker lace, holding energy drink; Bee: same chassis, now in matte-black stealth mode (non-reflective finish), eyes dimmed to near-black, wings still.
scenes.wardrobe: Leo wears same T-shirt and jeans, now with backpack; Jamal in navy varsity jacket; Priya in pleated skirt and cardigan; Tyler in graphic tee and joggers; Bee is matte-black with no visible glow until activation.
props: Lo-fi whale song waveform (brief holographic projection, cyan-green)
scenes.visual_style: Warm grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture — slight motion blur only on Bee’s zip-forward movement, crisp detail on facial reactions, realistic locker textures.
scenes.camera_setup: Hawk V-Lite anamorphic lens on locked-off tripod, 1/48s shutter speed
scenes.lighting_design: Mixed lighting: cool fluorescent overheads + warm natural light from tall windows; soft fill to reduce harsh shadows on faces; Bee’s white eye flare creates localized highlight on Tyler’s face during countdown.
scenes.audio_notes: Diegetic sounds: hallway chatter, locker slam, fluorescent buzz, Bee’s quick zip sound (high-frequency whoosh), Tyler’s shoelace scuff, Bee’s chirp, lo-fi whale song SFX (2 sec, layered with subtle reverb), eruption of laughter.
special_effects: Bee’s stealth mode (matte-black finish, zero reflectivity)
Holographic waveform projection (2-second cyan-green waveform, pulsing to beat)
scenes.transition_notes: CUT TO next scene — wide shot holds 2.3 seconds longer than scripted to let laughter settle before golden-hour garage light begins.
scenes.ai_generation_prompt: Static wide shot, 16:9, warm grounded realism, bustling high school hallway with metal lockers, fluorescent lights and tall sunlit windows, 15-year-old East Asian boy walking with two friends (one Black teen in varsity jacket, one South Asian teen with glasses), palm-sized matte-black bumblebee robot hovering discreetly at his shoulder, another teen smirking as he bumps the boy’s shoulder, robot zipping forward with white LED eye flare, target teen glancing down at untied shoelace and stumbling, brief cyan-green holographic waveform projection floating mid-air, shallow depth of field, cinematic color grade with balanced cool/warm lighting and realistic metal textures.
scenes.negative_prompt: empty hallway, cartoon characters, text, logos, motion blur on faces, unrealistic robot movement, glowing skin, overexposed windows, low resolution, noise, unrealistic reflections

scenes.title: The Garage — Sunset
scenes.summary: Golden hour light floods garage; Leo works on circuit board; Bee projects glowing schematics onto wall; Maya paints wings on plastic beetle; Bee speaks to Leo about dinner reminder and thermostat reprogramming; Leo looks at Bee, then reaches out; Bee lands on his palm, wings fold, light pulses slowly; Maya places painted beetle beside Bee, which glows faintly in response.
scenes.environment: Same garage as Scene 01, now bathed in golden hour light, tools organized, half-built prototypes on shelves, schematics projected onto cinderblock wall, paint supplies on secondary table, plastic beetle toy with fresh pink glitter paint.
scenes.character_descriptions: Leo: same as Scene 01, now sleeves rolled, focused expression; Maya: same as Scene 02, now holding paintbrush, glitter on nose; Bee: chrome chassis restored (no stealth mode), amber eyes pulsing slowly, wings articulated mid-fold.
scenes.wardrobe: Leo wears same T-shirt and jeans, sleeves rolled; Maya wears same striped leggings and sweater, now with paint-splattered apron.
props: Projected schematics (glowing blue-white vector lines on wall)
scenes.visual_style: Warm grounded realism with subtle sci-fi texture — rich golden highlights, deep warm shadows, precise focus on interplay of light on chrome, paint, and projection.
scenes.camera_setup: Hawk V-Lite anamorphic lens on locked-off tripod, 1/48s shutter speed
scenes.lighting_design: Golden hour sunlight streaming diagonally through garage windows, illuminating dust motes and creating long warm shadows; practical LED fill for schematic projection brightness; Bee’s amber pulse provides localized face illumination on Leo.
scenes.audio_notes: Diegetic sounds: soft soldering hiss, paintbrush strokes, Bee’s gentle hum, Mom’s off-screen voice saying 'dinner is in 12 minutes', Bee’s warm voice delivery, faint shimmer SFX on beetle glow response.
special_effects: Bee’s slow amber pulse (synchronized to heartbeat rhythm)
Plastic beetle bioluminescent response (subtle pink glow, triggered by proximity)
scenes.transition_notes: FADE OUT on final wide shot — golden light lingers, hum fades, then child’s laugh cuts in under title card.
scenes.ai_generation_prompt: Wide static shot, 16:9, warm grounded realism, golden hour light flooding cluttered suburban garage, 15-year-old East Asian boy soldering circuit board at workbench, 8-year-old East Asian girl painting pink glitter wings on plastic beetle toy, palm-sized chrome bumblebee robot projecting glowing blue-white vector schematics onto cinderblock wall, robot’s amber LED eyes pulsing slowly, both children smiling, shallow depth of field, anamorphic lens flare on sunlit chrome, cinematic color grade with rich amber highlights and deep warm shadows.
scenes.negative_prompt: night time, indoor lighting only, cartoon, text, logos, motion blur, shaky cam, overexposed highlights, unrealistic glow, floating objects, low resolution, noise, plastic toy looking like real insect

sound_requirements: Bee’s warm gender-neutral voice (recorded dry + with variable reverb)
Lo-fi whale song SFX (2 sec, layered reverb)
vfx_requirements: Practical LED eye states (amber/green/white pulse logic)
Holographic projections (gear, starfield, schematics, waveform) — all volumetric, soft-edged, with accurate light interaction
Matte-black stealth mode rendering (zero reflectivity, no subsurface scatter)

environments.name: Cluttered suburban garage with north-facing dusty windows, workbench covered in blueprints, soldering iron, salvaged electronics (circuit boards, wires, capacitors), metal toolboxes, ambient dust motes visible in light shafts.
environments.lighting: Natural afternoon sunlight slanting through high garage windows, creating strong directional beams with visible dust motes; soft fill bounce from white-painted walls; no artificial key lights.

environments.name: Warm, lived-in suburban kitchen with vintage appliances (chrome toaster, enamel fridge), morning light from large east-facing window, steam rising from oatmeal pot, glitter glue dots on table, crayons and paper scattered near Maya.
environments.lighting: Natural morning light from large east-facing window, soft and even; warm fill from overhead pendant light; gentle rim light on Bee’s chassis; oatmeal steam backlit by window.

environments.name: Teen bedroom with minimalist decor, blue LED strip lighting along baseboards and desk edge, corkboard with pinned sketches, bookshelf with physics textbooks and origami cranes, single paper crane spinning lazily near open window.
environments.lighting: Low-level blue LED strip lighting (no direct glare), soft ambient fill from unseen source, Bee’s amber eyes and hologram providing primary illumination on Leo’s face; ceiling starfield projection casting faint moving light on walls.

environments.name: Public high school hallway with lockers (metal, dented), fluorescent ceiling lights buzzing softly, natural light from tall vertical windows, backpacks slung over shoulders, posters on walls (science fair, basketball game), lunch tray clatter audible in distance.
environments.lighting: Mixed lighting: cool fluorescent overheads + warm natural light from tall windows; soft fill to reduce harsh shadows on faces; Bee’s white eye flare creates localized highlight on Tyler’s face during countdown.

environments.name: Same garage as Scene 01, now bathed in golden hour light, tools organized, half-built prototypes on shelves, schematics projected onto cinderblock wall, paint supplies on secondary table, plastic beetle toy with fresh pink glitter paint.
environments.lighting: Golden hour sunlight streaming diagonally through garage windows, illuminating dust motes and creating long warm shadows; practical LED fill for schematic projection brightness; Bee’s amber pulse provides localized face illumination on Leo.
